Dissertation Report · 2003

Antimicrobial Effects of Sodium Lactate (PURASAL®) as Ingredient in Ready to Eat Meat Product — Mortadella Beef

A dissertation report submitted by Ibrahim Al-Hassan

AuthorIbrahim Al-Hassan
Year2003
FieldFood Science & Nutrition
InstitutionColumbus University
Pages61
Download PDF Open in New Tab

Abstract

The study was to find out the effect of sodium lactate on ready to eat meat product (Mortadella beef) on the shelf life and its effect on pathogens. There was an increase in the shelf life of product in comparison to the control with no added Sodium lactate. Sodium lactate is found to be a inhibitor of microbes due to its effect in proton transport across membranes in the metabolism, also the increase in water binding capacity of sodium lactate. Inhibition of salmonella, Listeria, Staph aureus and total aerobic counts was significant from the use of Sodium lactate in comparison to the control. There was no effect on the chemical composition and also no significant change in sensory attributes of the product noticed in samples.

Keywords

Sodium Lactate PURASAL® Mortadella Beef Ready-to-Eat Meat Shelf Life Food Preservation Listeria monocytogenes Salmonella Staphylococcus aureus
